summary: Lead accepts M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 after gameplay_designer REVIEW, systems_architect ACCEPT, and independent qa_reviewer ACCEPT. The accepted artifact is a planning-only M5 playable-slice contract; it does not pass M5 formally, start downstream implementation, decide manual node battle, or alter product code, packages, content-source data, dependencies, release artifacts, roadmap, acceptance matrix, ADRs, gate docs, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, branding, commercial choices, frozen architecture/platform/core decisions, irreversible migrations, paid services/accounts/secrets, or formal historical content.

tests_run.result: VALID task file before lead acceptance update.
Branch diff is confined to PROJECT_STATUS.md, project/tasks/**, project/messages/**, project/goal-mode-state.json, and project/model-routing-state.json.

decisions: Accept the slice definition as a reviewed planning contract, not as formal M5 passage.
Move M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 to ACCEPTED pending PR/CI/main integration; do not mark CLOSED until the accepted branch enters main and local main is synchronized.
Keep downstream M5 implementation and evidence tasks DRAFT until this task is CLOSED and taskctl ready selects them.
Preserve all Human Gates listed in the task and QA/system reviews.
risks: The contract contains starting hypotheses for session length, map scale, in-game duration, AI duration, and comprehension metrics; downstream tasks must validate or revise them with evidence.

If downstream implementation discovers a missing protocol/save/schema/architecture surface, it must route through that task's systems review and any required ADR or Human Gate instead of silently expanding scope.
requested_action: Commit lead acceptance, open PR, wait for CI, merge into main, then close M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 and continue to the next dependency-ready M5 task.
